provenance_notes: Codex inspected public base-to-head diffs and authored claim_id, claim_type, claim_text, test_scope, and evidence_items.
cgpro provided decision guidance only; cgpro/provider output is not non-LLM evidence.
Yann has not independently reviewed each selected case after diff inspection, so these records keep human_review_required=true.

deterministic_partition_rule.rule: sort selected case_ids lexicographically; compute sha256('g6d1-holdout:' + case_id); lowest hash becomes holdout, others train

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #14420 (pytest 9.0.x backport), `pytest.raises(..., match=...)` suppresses the original exception context when the raised exception type matches but the regex match fails. The implementation raises `AssertionError(self._fail_reason) from None`, so short traceback output reports the regex mismatch without surfacing `ValueError: actual` or chained-exception boilerplate.
diff_notes: PR pytest-dev/pytest#14420 changes src/_pytest/raises.py:RaisesExc.__exit__ so match-failure AssertionError is raised `from None`, suppressing chained context from the originally raised exception.

New test testing/python/raises.py::TestRaises::test_raises_match_failure_suppresses_exception_context creates a ValueError('actual') under pytest.raises(..., match='expected'), runs pytest with --tb=short, asserts a regex mismatch AssertionError, and asserts the original ValueError plus chained-context messages are absent.

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #683 (sqlite-utils), CSV/TSV insert and upsert detect column types by default instead of requiring `--detect-types`. The CLI adds `--no-detect-types` as the opt-out path, and `insert_upsert_implementation()` wraps CSV/TSV docs in `TypeTracker` unless that opt-out flag is present.
diff_notes: PR sqlite-utils#683 changes sqlite_utils/cli.py: the CSV/TSV insert/upsert options keep --detect-types but add --no-detect-types, and the implementation now runs TypeTracker when no_detect_types is false, making detection the default.

tests/test_cli.py::test_insert_detect_types is rewritten to parametrize option in [None, -d, --detect-types] and assert inserted CSV rows produce integer age and float weight even when no detect-types flag is supplied.

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #681 (sqlite-utils), CLI commands that accept `--functions` can receive the option multiple times, and each supplied inline function definition is registered before SQL execution. The implementation makes the Click option `multiple=True` and iterates over every supplied definition in `_maybe_register_functions()`.
diff_notes: PR sqlite-utils#681 changes sqlite_utils/cli.py so --functions uses multiple=True on query/bulk/memory-style command surfaces and _maybe_register_functions(functions_list) loops over each supplied functions string before registration.

New test tests/test_cli.py::test_query_functions_multiple_invocations invokes query with two --functions values defining triple() and quadruple(), then asserts the SQL result contains both computed values.

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #763 (structlog), the stdlib caller-frame helper supports a `stacklevel` offset after structlog/logging frames have been skipped. `_find_first_app_frame_and_name()` accepts `stacklevel`, and stdlib `findCaller()` paths forward an adjusted stacklevel so caller reporting can skip additional application frames.
diff_notes: PR structlog#763 changes src/structlog/_frames.py to add stacklevel handling to _find_first_app_frame_and_name(), then changes src/structlog/stdlib.py findCaller paths to pass adjusted stacklevel values through the helper.

New test tests/test_frames.py::TestFindFirstAppFrameAndName::test_stacklevel builds a fake frame chain and asserts stacklevel=1 returns the expected caller frame after ignored structlog/additional frames are skipped.

deferred_diff_inspected_candidates.reason: Diff stat showed broad docs/python-api churn plus db/table/view behavior; deferred to keep G-6d.1 tranche narrow and simpler than the selected CLI/helper cases.
Diff is a valid future candidate (ConsoleRenderer columns can be got/set) but was deferred to avoid over-selecting structlog dev-renderer property cases in this four-case tranche.

metadata_reject_groups.reason: non-Python-adapter-dependent or packaging/runtime surface too broad for this tranche
requires known clone-helper carve-out or older requirements pattern
